summary: In cephalometric analysis, the sella-nasion plane serves as the primary reference when assessing growth patterns over time because it provides stable alignment relative to the skull’s basic structure. This makes it ideal for accurate comparison between serial radiographs during longitudinal assessments of skeletal maturation.
